FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Continued To Apr-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Trustee Thomas Lucas and the Attorney Sheila Robello for failure to file the First Account. The return date is April 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. If the First Account is not filed by close of business on April 19, 2010, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will be imposed against the Trustee and the Attorney starting on that date and will run until the account is filed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1447 22nd Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Oct-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for Thomas Lucas (Successor Trustee).

The sale was confirmed at $767,000.00. An additional bond is required. The amount is to be determined by Counsel. The Court granted the petition (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jung.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1447 22nd Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Jul-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Thomas Lucas (Successor Trustee). May Cam (Real Estate Agent for Coldwell Banker/Optima) in pro per.

The Court announced the location of the property on for sale and asked for overbids; no overbids were stated. Ms. Robello stated that the selling price was $745,000.00, presented the terms of the sale for the record, and informed the Court that the representative of Coldwell Banker wished to address the Court. May Cam, Agent for Coldwell Banker, stated that due to the recent death of the Conservatee in the property that her clients wished to withdraw from the sale. Ms. Robello stated that she was willing to let them withdraw. The Court ordered the matter off calendar.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Aguerre.

Petition For Authority To (1)Sell Real Property Held In Trust Located At 1447 22nd Avenue, San Francisco; And (2) Enter Into An Exclusive Right-To-Sell Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-25-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Appointing T.A. Lucas Successor Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Setting Trustee Bond.
Off Calendar Jan-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Appointing T.A. Lucas Successor Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Setting Trustee Bond.
Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Continued To Jan-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robelo for Thomas Lucas (Proposed Trustee). Marianne Poynor (Interested Party) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Poynor requested a continuance stating that she was unable to contact a Lawyer. Ms. Robelo stated that there was an urgency with the reverse mortgage, that the lender was pushing for sale of the real property due to mishandling of the proceeds and due to the terms of the reverse mortgage not being met. In addition, Ms. Robelo stated that Ms. Poynor represented to the reverse mortgage company that the Conservatee was residing in the home/property when he was already residing at the care facility, that there was a $90,000.00 tax deficiency and that there was a balance of $170,000.00 owed to the care facility. Ms. Poynor stated that she was objecting to Mr. Lucas being appointed as the Trustee. The matter was continued to January 21, 2009. The Court informed Ms. Poyner that her written objection was due two days prior to January 16, 2009 and that the petition would be granted at that time if the written objection is not filed.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

Petition For Order Appointing T.A. Lucas Successor Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Setting Trustee Bond.
